title: EIP-8178: Binary SSZ Transport for the Engine API
description: Specifies a binary SSZ transport as an alternative to JSON-RPC for Engine API communication between consensus layer and execution layer clients. Replaces JSON-RPC with resource-oriented REST endpoints and raw SSZ encoding, reducing payload sizes by approximately 50% and eliminating hex encoding and JSON parsing overhead on the critical path for block propagation and validation.
author: Giulio Rebuffo (@Giulio2002)
laymanDescription: Replaces the text-based messaging format between Ethereum's two main software components with a compact binary format. This cuts message sizes roughly in half and removes unnecessary conversion steps, making block processing faster without requiring any changes to how the network itself operates.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. Faster CL-EL communication may marginally improve block propagation times, but user-facing behavior is unchanged.

appDevs.description: No impact. The Engine API is internal to node operators; application developers do not interact with it directly.

walletDevs.description: No impact. Wallet software does not interact with the Engine API; this change is invisible to wallet developers.

toolingInfra.description: Debugging and monitoring tools that inspect Engine API traffic must add SSZ decoding support alongside existing JSON-RPC parsing.

layer2s.description: No direct impact. Layer 2 systems do not use the Engine API; any benefit from faster block propagation is indirect.

stakersNodes.description: Node operators gain an optional performance improvement by enabling binary SSZ transport; existing JSON-RPC remains available as a fallback.

clClients.description: Must implement an HTTP client that sends SSZ-encoded requests to REST endpoints instead of JSON-RPC, aligning with their native SSZ usage.

elClients.description: Must add REST endpoint routing, SSZ deserialization for all Engine API containers, and serve both transports on the existing Engine API port.

benefits: Reduces Engine API payload sizes by approximately 50% by eliminating hex encoding overhead.
Removes JSON parsing and generation CPU overhead from the block propagation critical path.
Eliminates redundant SSZ-to-JSON-to-internal-type conversion round-trips on both client sides.
Requires no hard fork; clients can adopt the new transport incrementally via regular releases.
tradeoffs: Increases implementation surface as clients must support two transports (JSON-RPC and SSZ REST) simultaneously.
Debugging becomes harder since binary SSZ traffic is not human-readable without specialized tooling.

scaleL1.description: Faster CL-EL communication reduces block propagation and validation latency, supporting higher throughput on L1.
